title: Binary Search
problem_statement: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target value, return the index if the target is found. Otherwise return -1.
constraints: nums is sorted in ascending order and contains distinct values.
intuition: Use binary search to halve the search interval until you find the target or the interval is empty.

approaches.name: Classic Binary Search
approaches.description: Maintain left and right bounds, compare the middle element with the target, and move one bound inward based on the comparison.

interview_qa.q: Why compute mid as left + (right - left) / 2?
interview_qa.a: This avoids integer overflow when left and right are large.
Stop when left > right, which means the target is not present in the interval.
